scope: GA4 web tracking-plan scenario library for human analysts and Codex-assisted tracking plan generation.
source_priority: Official Google documentation first. Trusted practitioner sources only for custom-event patterns and implementation judgement.
value_convention: For controlled analytics values, use lowercase ASCII snake_case, remove accents/diacritics, and list finite options with pipes. Preserve official IDs, ISO codes, numeric values, URLs, and raw native/user-entered fields only when required and privacy-safe.

sources.name: GA4 automatically collected events
sources.used_for: Automatic and enhanced-measurement web events and default parameters.

sources.name: GA4 recommended events
sources.used_for: Full recommended event catalog and event-level parameters.

sources.name: GA4 ecommerce measurement
sources.used_for: Ecommerce dataLayer/gtag examples and items array structure.

sources.name: GA4 event parameters
sources.used_for: Parameter reporting behavior for automatic, enhanced, and recommended events.

sources.name: GA4 event setup
sources.used_for: How recommended/custom events are configured with gtag.js or GTM.

sources.name: GTM data layer
sources.used_for: dataLayer purpose and dataLayer.push implementation rules.

sources.name: GA4 custom events
sources.used_for: Rule to prefer automatic/enhanced/recommended events before custom events.

sources.name: GA4 event collection limits
sources.used_for: Event naming length and event-parameter limits.

sources.name: Simo Ahava GA4 ecommerce guide
sources.used_for: Implementation judgement for GA4 ecommerce with GTM.

sources.name: Simo Ahava GA4 events implementation guide
sources.used_for: Custom-event design judgement and GA4 implementation considerations.

sources.name: Analytics Mania GA4 custom events
sources.used_for: Typical GTM custom-event patterns and reporting considerations.

sources.name: Analytics Mania GTM data layer tutorial
sources.used_for: Human explanation and practical dataLayer examples.

standard_events.trigger: Page load or browser history state change.
standard_events.implementation: Usually automatic. Manually control only when SPA/infinite-scroll behavior requires it.

standard_events.parameters: client_id, ga_session_id, ga_session_number, page_location, page_referrer, page_title

standard_events.trigger: User starts or resumes a session.
standard_events.parameters: client_id, ga_session_id, ga_session_number, page_location, page_referrer, page_title

standard_events.trigger: Webpage is in focus for at least one second.
First time a user reaches 90% vertical depth.
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Custom scroll_depth event only if 25/50/75 thresholds are needed.

standard_events.trigger: User clicks a link leading away from the current domain.
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Avoid duplicating with custom outbound_click unless needed.

standard_events.trigger: Search results page is shown based on configured query parameter.
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Use recommended search for explicit submitted-search tracking.

standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement when YouTube JS API support exists.

standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement when YouTube JS API support exists.

standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement when YouTube JS API support exists.

standard_events.trigger: User clicks a common downloadable file extension.
standard_events.parameters: file_extension, file_name, link_classes, link_id, link_text, link_url
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Custom file events only for gated downloads or special classifications.

standard_events.trigger: First time a user interacts with a form in a session.
standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Custom form_step events when multi-step funnel detail is required.
Enhanced measurement. Use generate_lead for validated lead success.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a user is awarded virtual currency in a game. Log this along with spend_virtual_currency to better understand your virtual economy.

parameters.name: virtual_currency_name
parameters.description: The name of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value of the virtual currency.

official_recommended_events.description: Log this event when a user joins a group such as a guild, team, or family. Use this event to analyze how popular certain groups or social features are.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: The ID of the group.

official_recommended_events.description: Send this event to signify that a user has logged in to your website or app.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method used to login.

official_recommended_events.description: Log this event to indicate when the user has performed a search. You can use this event to identify what users are searching for on your website or app. For example, you could send this event when a user views a search results page after performing a search.

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description: The term that was searched for.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies that a user has selected some content of a certain type. This event can help you identify popular content and categories of content on your website or app.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The type of selected content.

parameters.name: content_id
parameters.description: An identifier for the content that was selected. The value must include at least one non-numeric character.

official_recommended_events.description: Use this event when a user has shared content.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method in which the content is shared.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The type of shared content.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared content.

official_recommended_events.description: This event indicates that a user has signed up for an account. Use this event to understand the different behaviors of logged in and logged out users.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The method used for sign up.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures the sale of virtual goods in your app and helps you identify which virtual goods are the most popular.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: virtual_currency_name
parameters.description: The name of the virtual currency.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item the virtual currency is being used for.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies the start of the on-boarding process. Use this in a funnel with tutorial_complete to understand how many users complete the tutorial.
This event signifies the user's completion of your on-boarding process. Use this in a funnel with tutorial_begin to understand how many users complete the tutorial.
This event signifies a user has submitted their payment information in an ecommerce checkout process.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the event. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: The chosen method of payment.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items for the event.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: affiliation
parameters.description: A product affiliation to designate a supplying company or brick and mortar store location. Note: affiliation is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the item. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: discount
parameters.description: The unit monetary discount value associated with the item.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index/position of the item in a list.

parameters.name: item_brand
parameters.description: The brand of the item.

parameters.name: item_category
parameters.description: The category of the item. If used as part of a category hierarchy or taxonomy then this will be the first category.

parameters.name: item_category2
parameters.description: The second category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category3
parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category4
parameters.description: The fourth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category5
parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_list_id
parameters.description: The ID of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_id is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_id is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_list_name
parameters.description: The name of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_name is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_name is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.description: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.example: ChIJIQBpAG2ahYAR_6128GcTUEo (the Google Place ID for San Francisco)
parameters.description: The physical location associated with the item (e.g. the physical store location). It's recommended to use the Google Place ID that corresponds to the associated item. A custom location ID can also be used. Note: location_id is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The monetary unit price of the item, in units of the specified currency parameter. If a discount applies to the item, set price to the discounted unit price and specify the unit price discount in the discount parameter.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Item quantity. If not set, quantity is set to 1.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies when one or more items is purchased by a user.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: customer_type
parameters.description: Is the conversion from a `new` or `returning` customer? new: New customer who hasn't purchased within a given time period (540-day window recommended and set at default, but not required). returning: A returning customer who has purchased during the given time period. Don't specify a value if there's uncertainty (for example, if the user checked out as a guest).

parameters.name: transaction_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a transaction. The transaction_id parameter helps you avoid getting duplicate events for a purchase.

parameters.name: coupon
parameters.description: The coupon name/code associated with the event. Event-level and item-level coupon parameters are independent.

parameters.name: shipping
parameters.description: Shipping cost associated with a transaction.

parameters.name: tax
parameters.description: Tax cost associated with a transaction.

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: The items for the event.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.name: item_name
parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameters.description: The third category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_category4

parameters.description: The fifth category hierarchy or additional taxonomy for the item.

parameters.name: item_list_id
parameters.description: The ID of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_id is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_id is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_list_name
parameters.description: The name of the list in which the item was presented to the user. If set, event-level item_list_name is ignored. If not set, event-level item_list_name is used, if present.

parameters.name: item_variant
parameters.description: The item variant or unique code or description for additional item details/options.

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.example: ChIJIQBpAG2ahYAR_6128GcTUEo (the Google Place ID for San Francisco)
parameters.description: The physical location associated with the item (e.g. the physical store location). It's recommended to use the Google Place ID that corresponds to the associated item. A custom location ID can also be used. Note: location_id is only available at the item-scope.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: The monetary unit price of the item, in units of the specified currency parameter. If a discount applies to the item, set price to the discounted unit price and specify the unit price discount in the discount parameter.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Item quantity. If not set, quantity is set to 1.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies that an item was removed from a cart.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the items associated with the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * Value metrics on the view_item event to not contribute to revenue * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * Set value to the sum of (price * quantity) for all items in items. Don't include shipping or tax. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.description: The items for the event.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies that a player has reached the end of a level in a game.

parameters.name: level_name
parameters.description: The name of the level.

parameters.name: success
parameters.description: Set to true if the level was completed successfully.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies that a player has started a level in a game.

parameters.name: level_name
parameters.description: The name of the level.

official_recommended_events.description: This event signifies that a player has leveled up in a game. Use it to gauge the level distribution of your user base and identify levels that are difficult to complete.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The level of the character.

parameters.name: character
parameters.description: The character that leveled up.

official_recommended_events.description: Send this event when the user posts a score. Use this event to understand how users are performing in your game and correlate high scores with audiences or behaviors.

parameters.name: score
parameters.description: The score to post.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The level for the score.

parameters.name: character
parameters.description: The character that achieved the score.

official_recommended_events.description: Log this event when the user has unlocked an achievement. This event can help you understand how users are experiencing your game.

parameters.name: achievement_id
parameters.description: The id of the achievement that was unlocked.

official_recommended_events.description: Log this event when a qualified lead is successfully converted into a customer. This typically signifies the end of the lead nurturing process, such as when a contract is signed, a paid subscription starts, or a final sale is completed.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value associated with the conversion (such as contract value), in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. Required if value is set.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the conversion, such as first payment amount or contract value. Required if currency is set.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a user is marked as not becoming a converted lead, along with the reason.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: unconvert_lead_reason
parameters.description: The reason the lead was unconverted.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a user is marked as disqualified to become a lead, along with the reason for the disqualification.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: disqualified_lead_reason
parameters.description: The reason a lead was marked as disqualified.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a lead has been generated (for example, through a form). Log this to understand the effectiveness of your marketing campaigns and how many customers re-engage with your business after remarketing to the customers.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: The source of the lead.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a user is marked as meeting the criteria to become a qualified lead.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

official_recommended_events.description: This event measures when a user contacts or is contacted by a representative.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the value of the event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format. * If you set value then currency is required for revenue metrics to be computed accurately.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The monetary value of the event. * value is typically required for meaningful reporting. If you mark the event as a key event then it's recommended you set value. * currency is required if you set value.

parameters.name: lead_status
parameters.description: The status of the lead.

typical_custom_events.scenario: insurance, banking, telco, services quote funnels
typical_custom_events.use_when: User starts a quote flow but no lead has been generated yet.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead after a successful lead/quote submission.

typical_custom_events.scenario: multi-step lead, signup, booking, checkout-like forms
typical_custom_events.use_when: A form step is displayed and enhanced measurement is too coarse.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use form_start/form_submit when only generic form engagement is needed.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A step is completed before final submission.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead/sign_up/purchase when the final business outcome occurs.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User sees a validation or system error that affects conversion.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: No direct GA4 recommended event fits. Avoid raw error text if it may contain PII.

typical_custom_events.scenario: listing pages, search results, catalogs, real estate, jobs
typical_custom_events.use_when: A filter materially changes the displayed results.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use view_search_results/search if the interaction is a search query.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User clicks a contact CTA but does not submit a lead form.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead once a valid lead/contact form is submitted.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User clicks account/login entry point before authentication.
User starts a live chat or bot conversation.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead if chat creates a qualified lead.
Use generate_lead if result submission creates a lead.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User selects a plan before checkout/signup.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use begin_checkout/purchase if the plan selection enters a checkout.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A key product feature is used and no recommended event fits.

typical_custom_events.scenario: healthcare, services, local business, real estate
typical_custom_events.use_when: User starts an appointment booking flow.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead when appointment request is submitted.
Use generate_lead if booked appointment is modeled as a lead.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A modal materially changes the journey or blocks/provides conversion path.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content if it is simply content selection.

typical_custom_events.use_when: FAQ interaction is a real support/reassurance KPI.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content if treating FAQ selection as content selection is sufficient.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: media site, blog, documentation, editorial brand
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, scroll, select_content, share, file_download, video_start, video_progress, video_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: page_data.template, content_type, content_id, content_name, link_url, video_title
scenario_playbooks.notes: Prefer enhanced measurement for scroll/video/download when it is sufficient; use select_content for content card/article selections.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: insurance, banking, B2B services, SaaS demo, contact forms
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, search, form_start, form_submit, generate_lead, qualify_lead, disqualify_lead, working_lead, close_convert_lead, close_unconvert_lead
scenario_playbooks.typical_custom_events: begin_quote, form_step_view, form_step_submit, form_error, contact_intent, calculator_start, calculator_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: lead_source, form_name, form_step, product_type, quote_entry_point, cta_location
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use generate_lead only after a valid submission or qualified lead action, not on CTA click.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: view_item_list, select_item, view_item, add_to_wishlist, add_to_cart, remove_from_cart, view_cart, begin_checkout, add_shipping_info, add_payment_info, purchase, refund
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: currency, value, transaction_id, items, items[].item_id, items[].item_name, items[].item_category, items[].price, items[].quantity
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use official ecommerce event names and items array. Clear ecommerce object before ecommerce pushes when using GTM/dataLayer.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: home page offers, banners, campaign modules, cross-sell modules
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: promotion_id, promotion_name, creative_name, creative_slot, items, items[].item_id, items[].item_name
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use promotion events only for real promotions/offers, not every generic content card.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: sign_up, login, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete, join_group, share, select_content, purchase
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use login/sign_up for successful actions; use intent custom events for pre-auth clicks or plan exploration.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: site search, real estate, jobs, product/category listings
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: search_term, result_count, filter_category, filter_value, sort_type, item_list_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use search for submitted queries and view_search_results when results page detection via query parameter is enough.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: search, view_item_list, select_item, view_item, begin_checkout, add_payment_info, purchase, generate_lead
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: destination, dates, guests_count, item_id, item_name, value, currency, transaction_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Model paid bookings as ecommerce when money is transacted; model appointment requests as leads.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: help center, claims, account support, chat
scenario_playbooks.notes: Do not send claim descriptions, policy numbers, email, phone, or chat text to GA4.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: view_item_list, select_item, view_item, sign_up, begin_checkout, purchase, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: course_id, course_name, content_type, progress_percent, value, currency
scenario_playbooks.notes: Paid enrollment can use ecommerce. Learning engagement may need custom events.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: web game, mobile game, gamified platform
scenario_playbooks.official_events: earn_virtual_currency, spend_virtual_currency, level_start, level_end, level_up, post_score, unlock_achievement, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: level_name, success, score, character, virtual_currency_name, value, achievement_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use official game recommended events wherever possible.

datalayer_patterns.name: Generic interaction event
datalayer_patterns.use_for: Custom interactions that are not ecommerce.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Custom Event trigger = custom_event_name. GA4 Event tag event name = {{Event}} or fixed event name. Map event_data.* as event parameters.

datalayer_patterns.name: Manual page_view with page_data
datalayer_patterns.use_for: SPA route changes or explicit pageview control.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Trigger on page_view. Map page_data.location/title/template/language into GA4 parameters when needed.

datalayer_patterns.name: Recommended search event
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = search. Map search_term and optional custom result_count.

datalayer_patterns.name: Lead success event
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = generate_lead. Map lead_source and business parameters. Mark as key event if appropriate.

datalayer_patterns.name: Custom quote start event
datalayer_patterns.use_for: User starts quote flow before a lead exists.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = begin_quote. Register key dimensions only if needed for reporting.

datalayer_patterns.name: Ecommerce add_to_cart
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = add_to_cart. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object, for example currency from ecommerce.currency and items from ecommerce.items.

datalayer_patterns.name: Ecommerce purchase
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = purchase. transaction_id is required for deduplication/reporting quality. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object.

datalayer_patterns.name: Promotion impression/click
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Use view_promotion for exposure and select_promotion for click, keeping promotion IDs stable across both. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object.
